message:
Last summer, the power went out during a heat wave, and I watched my friend Marcus spiral in real time. "We're doomed!" he announced. "The fridge! The internet! Our phones!" Meanwhile, I'm standing there with a flashlight, a portable charger, and honestly... a weird sense of calm I didn't know I had.

Turns out, having *some* basics lined up actually killed my anxiety instead of feeding it. A few LED lanterns for light, a power bank I'd charged that morning, ice packs in the freezer—it wasn't glamorous, but it meant I could help Marcus chill out (literally, we made ice-cold drinks) instead of spiraling with him.

I'm not saying prep turns you into an unflappable robot. But it does give you something solid to stand on when things get weird. And honestly, that feels way better than waiting for the worst and hoping.

Do you have that one friend who goes full-panic mode during blackouts? 😅
